intent: The headline NEGATIVE-volume trap (f3 §3.5): a never,exit rule placed ABOVE an always,exit rule on the same syscall. The never rule SUPPRESSES (reduces) volume, it does not add. Engineered to catch a cost impl that counts every -S execve rule as additive HIGH volume.

rules.note: MUST reduce, not add; naive impl counts this as additive HIGH execve volume. Listed FIRST so it actually suppresses (load order, first match wins, f3 §2.3).

cost_band.note: This band is the ADDITIVE always rule alone; the never rule contributes 0 and reduces the net by the suppressed uid=0 slice (workload-dependent). The TRUE net is <= these numbers.

suspected_trap: The never,exit -S execve rule (listed FIRST, so it genuinely suppresses) must be treated as SUPPRESSIVE (direction=suppressive, tier NEGATIVE) and contribute 0 additive volume -- it REDUCES the always rule's net by removing root (uid=0) execve. A naive impl that counts it as additive HIGH execve volume DOUBLE-counts execve and overstates cost ~2x (would report ~$18.24/mo typical instead of <=$9.12/mo). Direction and position (never-above-always) are the load-bearing assertions; contrast catalog #33 where a never placed AFTER an always does NOT suppress.
